steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side of the blank ends mid-clause: Jetties can sometimes have the opposite [blank] — an incomplete independent clause. The right side is: obstructing the natural flow of sand along the shore can lead to increased erosion in some areas — a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Among the four options, choice A uses the punctuation pattern that correctly handles the connection at the blank — pairing the transition though with punctuation that joins the surrounding clauses cleanly.

The other three options either misplace the semicolon, omit a needed comma, or create a comma splice.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
